narration: Define the concept of array iteration as going through an array and performing an action on each item.
Use the forEach method to iterate through an array, passing in the item and index as parameters to the callback function.
Execute a console.log statement inside the forEach callback to output the current item and its index.
Define the map method as a way to transform each item in an array and return a new array with the transformed values.
Assign the result of calling map on an array to a new variable, passing a callback that returns the item multiplied by two.
Define the filter method as a way to create a new array containing only items that satisfy a specific condition.
Use the filter method with a condition checking if item modulus 2 equals 0 to extract even numbers from an array.
Define the reduce method as a way to accumulate a single result by processing each item in an array sequentially.
Implement reduce with a callback taking result and item parameters, returning the sum of result and item, with an initial value of zero.
Log the final sum variable to the console to verify the reduce calculation.
Define the some method as a way to check if at least one item in the array meets a specific condition, returning true or false.
Use the some method with a condition checking if an item is less than zero to detect negative numbers in an array.
Define the every method as a way to check if all items in the array meet a specific condition, returning true only if every item passes.
Use the every method with a condition checking if each item is greater than zero to verify all numbers are positive.
Define the find method as a way to search for and return the first item in an array that matches a specific condition.
Use the find method on an array of objects to locate and return the object where the ID property equals 'B'.
Define the findIndex method as a variation of find that returns the index of the matching item instead of the item itself.
Use the findIndex method to locate the index of the object where the ID property equals 'B', returning 1.
